spec_match.rationale: The concept now explains policy-container inheritance and evaluates worker requests against inherited response-origin patterns rather than implying a worker-side API can set the policy.

interactive.rationale: The page includes editable patterns and requests, disposition and worker-context selects, and presets for attempted bypass and empty-policy scenarios.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index promise that workers inherit the document allowlist is demonstrated by dedicated, shared, and service worker context simulations.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo includes unsupported-browser fallback messaging, malformed worker URLs, empty policy behavior, report-only mode, and an attempted worker policy bypass.

references.rationale: The references section links to chromestatus.com/feature/5175745573945344 and the WICG draft spec.

design_system.rationale: The edited page uses shared CSS variables and pairs local button background overrides with explicit colors.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Rebuilt as a policy-container propagation simulator with inherited worker request evaluation and no remaining major findings.
